claim: Design is the primary differentiator in software when artificial intelligence accelerates the rate of feature creation and commodifies functionality.
actionable: Invest heavily in design systems and visual cohesion rather than feature parity; prioritize craft and taste as competitive advantages in AI-augmented product development.
source_title: Taste is your Moat (Dylan Field of Figma)

claim: Natural language will be the dominant interface for exploring latent space in the near term, but it is an early phase comparable to the MS-DOS era of computing, and more constrained, intuitive interfaces will eventually emerge.
actionable: When building AI products, expect that constrained UIs with lower-dimensional parameter controls will outperform pure text prompting as models mature and usage patterns solidify.
source_title: Taste is your Moat (Dylan Field of Figma)

claim: The blank canvas problem is a significant barrier to adoption in design tools; starting with an AI-generated prototype dramatically lowers friction compared to expecting users to ideate from scratch.
actionable: In design and creation tools, provide AI-powered starter templates or generation-first workflows to reduce user intimidation and activate the productive loop faster.
source_title: Taste is your Moat (Dylan Field of Figma)

claim: Better code generation increases the importance of design, not decreases it, because code generation commodifies basic UI implementation but leaves the strategic visual and experiential decisions to humans.
actionable: Use AI code generation to automate boilerplate, but allocate your design team's effort to system thinking, aesthetic direction, and user experience patterns that code alone cannot produce.
source_title: Taste is your Moat (Dylan Field of Figma)

claim: Agents writing code will eventually mean developers are unfamiliar with the code they deploy, requiring higher-level visual or abstract interfaces to reason about system behavior.
actionable: Begin preparing design abstractions and visual representations for agent-generated codebases so developers can audit and modify agent decisions without reading generated code directly.
source_title: Taste is your Moat (Dylan Field of Figma)

claim: Figma Make enables Figma to act as a context repository for aesthetics, allowing any AI model or agent downstream to inherit design system constraints and brand taste.
actionable: Establish Figma as your single source of truth for visual design rules and use Figma Make or similar tools to embed design context into agent code generation pipelines.
source_title: Taste is your Moat (Dylan Field of Figma)

claim: Role blurring and status blurring are inevitable as design tools enable non-designers to create high-fidelity outputs; designers' roles should shift toward stewarding others through the design process and raising the design ceiling.
actionable: Restructure design teams as mentors and system thinkers rather than individual contributors; focus on craft, patterns, and brand guidance to maintain quality as creation democratizes.
source_title: Taste is your Moat (Dylan Field of Figma)

claim: GPT-3 was Dylan Field's first clear signal that something exponential was happening with AI scaling, not just hype, because the delta from previous models was visibly discontinuous.
actionable: When evaluating new AI models, look for discontinuous leaps in capability rather than incremental improvements as signals of fundamental breakthroughs.
source_title: Taste is your Moat (Dylan Field of Figma)

claim: The fast fashion model in software (rapid, disposable prototyping) is not dominant today; substantial software still requires expertise, intention, and knowledge of domain workflows that AI alone cannot capture.
actionable: Do not assume all software becomes disposable; invest in deep domain knowledge and human expertise for problems involving complex workflows, business logic, or regulatory constraints.
source_title: Taste is your Moat (Dylan Field of Figma)

claim: Start with dream and possibility, not critique; only after imagining what could be should you think through risks and mitigations.
actionable: When evaluating new ideas or investments, begin by asking 'how big could this be?' and 'how important could this be?' before assessing obstacles.
source_title: Taste is your Moat (Dylan Field of Figma)

claim: Early-stage recruiting requires viewing it as a sales funnel that must be continuously fed; thinking about recruiting throughout the day and acting on opportunities is more effective than dedicated recruiting time.
actionable: Maintain a recruiting spreadsheet visible daily; identify potential hires during every coffee, snack, or email break; prioritize relationship-building over hard selling.
source_title: Taste is your Moat (Dylan Field of Figma)

claim: The NFT cycle taught that hype cycles shift from idealism and vision to get-rich-quick mentality, and the same pattern is beginning in AI with passive income optimization content.
actionable: Be cautious of emerging AI spaces that shift from research-focused discussion to money-making hype; this is a leading indicator of market saturation and scam activity.
source_title: Taste is your Moat (Dylan Field of Figma)

claim: Digital scarcity (NFTs, collectibles) will persist for the small subset of humans with collector instincts, independent of generative AI abundance.
actionable: Design two parallel product streams: one for abundant, customized-to-user experiences, and one for intentionally scarce, collectible digital items for those with curator/collector psychology.
source_title: Taste is your Moat (Dylan Field of Figma)
